title: Sour Velvety Moroccan Eggplant Tagine
description: A crowd-pleasing, vegetarian-friendly Moroccan dish featuring tender eggplant in a rich, sour, and velvety sauce, thickened with potato starch. This recipe is a perfect blend of flavors and textures, sure to delight your taste buds.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the cubed eggplant and cook, stirring occasionally, until it starts to soften, about 5 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the potato starch, lemon juice, and a splash of the vegetable broth until smooth.
Add the starch mixture, diced tomatoes, salt, and black pepper to the pot and stir to combine.
Pour in the remaining vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, until the eggplant is tender and the sauce has thickened, about 20-25 minutes.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs or a dollop of yogurt, if desired.
